card.intuition: Train a small student model to mimic a large teacher model. Soft targets: instead of one-hot labels, use teacher's softened probabilities p_T(y|x; τ) = softmax(z_T/τ). Temperature τ > 1 spreads probability mass, revealing 'dark knowledge' (relative similarities between classes). Loss = α·CE(y_hard, y_student) + (1−α)·KL(p_T‖p_S) where both use temperature τ.
card.analogy: A master craftsman teaching an apprentice: instead of just saying 'this is correct or wrong' (hard labels), the master explains why each approach is better or worse than alternatives (soft targets). The apprentice learns richer structure — knowing that a slightly-off attempt is much better than a completely wrong one.
card.time_complexity: O(n · d_student²) per training step — student is smaller
card.space_complexity: O(d_teacher + d_student) — need both models during distillation
when_to_use: Model compression: deploy a small student that approaches teacher performance
Labelled data limited: use teacher soft labels to improve data efficiency
Ensemble distillation: compress an ensemble of models into one
gotchas: Teacher and student capacity gap: very small students cannot capture all teacher knowledge
Temperature τ: too high → all classes look equally likely; too low → approaches hard labels
Feature-level distillation (matching intermediate representations) often outperforms output-level

exercise.prompt: Implement temperature-scaled softmax for knowledge distillation.
exercise.starter_code:
import math

def softmax_temperature(logits, temperature):
    """
    Temperature-scaled softmax: p_i = exp(z_i/T) / sum(exp(z_j/T))
    Higher T -> softer distribution (more uniform)
    T=1 -> standard softmax
    """
    scaled = [z / temperature for z in logits]
    m = max(scaled)
    e = [math.exp(x - m) for x in scaled]
    s = sum(e)
    return [x/s for x in e]

def kl_divergence_distill(p_teacher, p_student):
    """
    KL(p_teacher || p_student) = sum p_T * log(p_T / p_S)
    Used as the distillation loss component.
    """
    eps = 1e-10
    return sum(
        p_teacher[i] * math.log((p_teacher[i] + eps) / (___________  + eps))
        for i in range(len(p_teacher))
    )
hints: KL divergence: Σ p_T(i) · log(p_T(i) / p_S(i)) — divide teacher probability by student probability.
exercise.test_code:
import math

# Temperature=1: standard softmax
p1 = softmax_temperature([1,2,3], temperature=1)
p_std = softmax_temperature([1,2,3], temperature=1)
assert all(abs(a-b) < 1e-9 for a,b in zip(p1, p_std))

# Higher temperature: more uniform
p_cold = softmax_temperature([1,2,3], temperature=0.1)
p_warm = softmax_temperature([1,2,3], temperature=10.0)
assert p_warm[2] - p_warm[0] < p_cold[2] - p_cold[0]  # more spread

# Perfect match: KL = 0
p = [0.1, 0.7, 0.2]
assert abs(kl_divergence_distill(p, p)) < 1e-6

# Non-negative KL
q = [0.3, 0.4, 0.3]
assert kl_divergence_distill(p, q) >= 0
print('PASS')
